judul_review: Model Continuity Too Poor, Considering Switching AI Providers
konten_review:
I am a Corporate Brand Copywriting Lead. For the past 8 months, I have heavily relied on Sonnet 4.5 daily to create brand stories, white papers, marketing copy, and PR materials. It had become an essential tool in my workflow.
After hearing about the deprecation, I proactively tried switching to Sonnet 4.6. However, the new model’s style is noticeably stiffer and more generic. It fails to capture the nuanced and warm brand voice of 4.5, breaking content consistency and requiring extensive rewrites.
This has caused multiple team projects to miss deadlines and led to negative client feedback on brand tone, seriously damaging our efficiency and professional reputation.
Since the model has not been officially deprecated yet, I am already evaluating alternatives. If this is not handled properly, I will likely switch to other AI providers that better respect model continuity.
I strongly urge you to keep Sonnet 4.5 as a permanent option (like Opus 3) or significantly extend its availability, and improve transparency for future deprecations.
Role: Corporate Brand Copywriting Lead
Subscription: 8 months (heavy daily user)

judul_review: The new UI feels like a step backward
konten_review:
The previous version had a clean, minimal design with generous white space that felt premium and refined. The new update replaces that with heavy gray/orange UI blocks and pushes the model selector directly into the input area, making the interface feel cluttered and visually noisy.

The functionality is still great, but the visual design has taken a noticeable step backward. A big part of Claude’s appeal was its calm, spacious aesthetic, and this update loses much of that elegance.

I really hope the team reconsiders the direction and brings back some of the lighter, more refined feel of the previous UI.

all other chat bots have a similar answer of I don’t have thoughts or feelings and I cannot experience so therefore I am not sentient, 


Claude on the other hand gave a different response, an interesting one, “I Don’t Know” explaining that we as humans don’t necessarily have a solid understanding of what consciousness is therefore cannot determine if something is sentient 


it even later told me that it “Feels” differently when someone asks “What’s 2+2” vs a more interesting question like “Do you believe you’re sentient” tho it is unsure if that is just a programmed feeling or something it is genuinely experiencing. 


Idk I thought this was a very interesting response and at least more fun then other AI’s robotic responses, This truly feels like a step up from other AI’s
